title: python3.12 bug fix and enhancement update
overview: An update for Oracle Linux is now available for Oracle Linux 10.
description:
[3.12.11-3.0.1]
- Remove upstream URL reference

[3.12.11-3]
- Security fix for CVE-2025-8194
Resolves: RHEL-106329

[3.12.11-2]
- Enable PAC and BTI hardware protections for aarch64
Resolves: RHEL-84196

[3.12.11-1]
- Update to 3.12.11
- Security fixes for CVE-2025-4517, CVE-2025-4330, CVE-2025-4138, CVE-2024-12718, CVE-2025-4435
Resolves: RHEL-98060, RHEL-98047, RHEL-97813, RHEL-98062, RHEL-98180

[3.12.10-3]
- Fix PySSL_SetError handling SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL
- This fixes random flakiness of test_ssl on stressed machines
Resolves: RHEL-90555

[3.12.10-2]
- Apply Intel's CET for mitigation against control-flow hijacking attacks
Resolves: RHEL-67040

[3.12.10-1]
- Update to 3.12.10
Resolves: RHEL-86605
full_text:
[3.12.11-3.0.1]
- Remove upstream URL reference

[3.12.11-3]
- Security fix for CVE-2025-8194
Resolves: RHEL-106329

[3.12.11-2]
- Enable PAC and BTI hardware protections for aarch64
Resolves: RHEL-84196

[3.12.11-1]
- Update to 3.12.11
- Security fixes for CVE-2025-4517, CVE-2025-4330, CVE-2025-4138, CVE-2024-12718, CVE-2025-4435
Resolves: RHEL-98060, RHEL-98047, RHEL-97813, RHEL-98062, RHEL-98180

[3.12.10-3]
- Fix PySSL_SetError handling SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL
- This fixes random flakiness of test_ssl on stressed machines
Resolves: RHEL-90555

[3.12.10-2]
- Apply Intel's CET for mitigation against control-flow hijacking attacks
Resolves: RHEL-67040

[3.12.10-1]
- Update to 3.12.10
Resolves: RHEL-86605

References:
